level_signals: Think like a GM with strong product and business instincts

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 6+ years product management experience, 3+ years building B2B products
Have 6+ years product management experience, 3+ years building B2B products
Are excited by bringing clarity to ambiguous problems
Enjoy wearing different hats in a lean environment
Think like a GM with strong product and business instincts
Communicate clearly and concisely, both verbally and in writing
Lead with curiosity, understanding, and a growth mindset
Work well cross-functionally (eg. sales, support, finance, marketing, leadership)
Will do what it takes to make your product and team successful

scope.surface: Connectivity — customer transaction data ingestion into Anrok, integration partner ecosystem, self-serve integrations, and financial analysis capabilities
core_responsibilities: Identify the next frontier of product and business opportunities for Anrok
Make integrations self-serve for faster customer onboarding
Build and scale our integration partner ecosystem to reach more customers
Define the strategy for serving customer financial analysis needs with Anrok data

green_flags: Anrok has strong customer traction (40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies, 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies)
Backed by top-tier investors including Sequoia, Spark, Index, and Khosla Ventures with $100M+ raised
Clear product ownership area defined (connectivity / integrations)
Generous benefits package: 100% medical/dental/vision, learning stipend, home office setup
Multiple office hubs offer geographic flexibility (SF, NYC, SLC, Dublin)
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Hybrid 3 days/week in-office requirement across only 4 hub cities — eliminates fully remote candidates
Nice-to-haves section is absent; all requirements framed as must-haves, leaving little signal on what's truly differentiating
JD does not mention integrations or technical background explicitly in requirements despite the role being 'Connectivity' — some ambiguity on how technical this PM needs to be

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years PM and GM framing)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from hybrid 3-days in-office requirement across named hubs)
ambiguities: No explicit 'Required' vs 'Preferred' distinction in the JD; all items under 'What excites us' are treated as hard must-haves per extraction rules, but some (e.g. cultural_fit items) may be soft in practice.

The role title is 'Connectivity' and responsibilities involve integrations, but no technical skills (e.g. APIs, data pipelines) are explicitly listed as requirements — ambiguous on expected technical depth.
Dublin office listed — unclear if this is an Ireland location and whether EU candidates are eligible or if work auth requirements differ by hub.
